title: Pentaphyllum hippianum
taxonomy: Pentaphyllum hippianum belongs to the family Celastraceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This plant is native to the Himalayan region and is often found growing in mountainous areas. From a taxonomic standpoint, Pentaphyllum hippianum is a member of the genus Pentaphyllum, which is characterized by its unique five-parted leaves. The species is also known for its distinctive flowers, which are typically yellow in color and arranged in clusters. Further classification of Pentaphyllum hippianum is within the order Celastrales, a group of plants that are often shrub-like in appearance.
uses: Pentaphyllum hippianum is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. Its medicinal uses include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to confirm its full range of medicinal applications.

parts: Pentaphyllum hippianum, also known as Himalayan prickly ash, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The medicinal parts of this plant are mainly its roots and bark, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
